Authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Desktop). Session 86b7a3a0-7b14-4bd1-b707-52c5741aaeeb.

Related: #10611

## PR-A scope: docs-immediate (substrate corrective follows in PR-B)

@tobiu corrected the strategic semantic of Sunset + Auto-Wakeup verbatim 2026-05-02 morning: *"sunset is supposed to be final. meaning: all three harnesses CAN start fresh sessions easily. the goal is not to drop more messages into the sunsetted session, but to start a fresh one. ideally with a first prompt to read and follow the agents startup instructions first."*

PR #10610 (merged earlier today) added cross-references framing sunset as *"no longer terminal for identities with a configured recovery route"* — empirically wrong per the operator-intent correction. Live docs on dev currently mislead any agent reading them.

This PR is the **immediate docs correction** per @neo-gpt's split-strategy proposal (don't gate the urgent semantic fix behind multi-cycle substrate work). The substrate behavior on `dev` does NOT yet match the corrected docs — `resumeHarness.mjs` currently performs Q1a in-place wake injection for ALL identities, NOT fresh-session-spawn. The substrate corrective is tracked in #10611, follow-up PR-B.

## What PR-A actually does

PR-A documents the **current transition state explicitly** + signals PR-B as the substrate corrective. It does NOT describe a future-only substrate; it describes the dev-state-as-of-merge truthfully (Q1a in-place injection shipped, Q1b fresh-session-spawn pending) AND surfaces the operator-intent semantic ("sunset is terminal for the old transcript") so agents reading docs do not interpret the current Q1a behavior as the canonical execution model.

## Diff: 2 files, +2 / -2 lines

Both files replace one substrate-context callout block with a corrected one. The corrected block contains the verbatim transition-state acknowledgment so any agent reading these docs at boot sees the truth-state, not the intent-state alone.

**`AGENTS.md` §14 PRE-DECISION SUNSET GATE Cross-reference clause:**
- BEFORE: *"sunset is no longer terminal for identities with a configured recovery route"*
- AFTER: includes the corrected operator-intent + explicit `**Substrate transition state:**` clause naming `current dev still performs Q1a in-place wake injection for ALL identities; the fresh-session corrective ships via #10611 PR-B` + `**Stale-wake discipline during transition:**` invariant directing agents to treat Q1a-shaped wakes in OLD sunsetted transcripts as noise (fresh session is canonical execution target).

**`.agents/skills/session-sunset/references/session-sunset-workflow.md` §1 substrate-context callout:**
- BEFORE: *"sunset is no longer terminal in the post-#10602 era for identities with a configured recovery route"*
- AFTER: same transition-state framing + same stale-wake invariant + identity-coverage scope (named coverage: `@neo-gemini-3-1-pro` shipped, `@neo-opus-4-7` pending PR-B, `@neo-gpt` deferred until Codex Desktop verified).

"Recovery is a safety net, NOT a license" framing preserved in both files.

## Why PR-A separately from PR-B

The docs are the public contract that agents read at boot. Leaving the post-#10610 wording on dev means every agent boot during the PR-B work cycle reads false guidance ("sunset is no longer terminal"). PR-A closes the agent-facing semantic gap immediately; PR-B closes the substrate-behavior gap on its own multi-cycle review surface without blocking the urgent docs fix.

## Test Evidence
Pure documentation; no runtime impact, no test surface. Manual verification:
- Re-read both edited sections post-fix; framing matches @tobiu's verbatim correction + names current Q1a behavior + names PR-B as corrective.
- Cross-references intact (#10564, #10596, #10602, #10607, #10610, #10611 ticket states verified).
- Identity-scope discipline matches current dev coverage.
- Stale-wake invariant reads as a positive directive ("treat as noise") not as ambiguity, preventing the legitimate-vs-stale-wake inference loop in the transition window.

## Cross-family mandate
Pure documentation, no runtime impact — qualifies for **micro-change exemption** per `.agents/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md` §6.1. Single-peer review request follows per §6.2.

## Provenance
- Operator-intent correction: @tobiu's verbatim 2026-05-02 morning prompt (cited in scope).
- Independent v-b-a convergence: @neo-gpt and Opus surfaced the same correction within ~8 seconds via separate threads at 10:14Z; Gemini aligned in subsequent broadcast.
- Cycle-of-failures memory anchor: `feedback_substrate_semantic_recalibration.md` candidate — when operator-intent for substrate is unclear, the highest-leverage v-b-a check is to ASK the operator BEFORE shipping.
